summary: Regression-only run after iteration_2 code-review tweaks (verify_password narrowed exceptions, hash_password typed bytes intermediate, create_alert -> None hint). Re-executed full pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. All 43 tests passed in 14.32s. No behavior change observed. Auth cookies (HttpOnly), role_overridden flag, logout, products CRUD, status engine, duplicate detection (409 + alert), threshold gates, alerts, reports, OCR endpoint all verified green.
critical_code_review_comments: verify_password() now catches only (ValueError, TypeError) — good narrowing. If passlib/bcrypt raises an UnknownHashError or other passlib-specific exception in the future, login could 500. Consider also catching passlib.exc.UnknownHashError or keeping a broad fallback for robustness.
create_alert() return type hint -> None added — fine. No behavior impact.
hash_password() explicit bytes typing — fine. No behavior impact.
Frontend was significantly refactored per the request note; scope here was backend-only so not re-validated end-to-end via Playwright.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@inventory.com / Admin@12345 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md and env defaults)
seed_data_creation: None — suite uses its own TEST_ prefixed register/products and self-cleans via test_cleanup_test_products.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite (43 tests) is the source of truth and is fully green. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Admin creds from /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Backend changes in this iteration are pure type-hint / narrower exception handling and have zero observable effect on the HTTP contract. If a future change touches verify_password again, double-check passlib exception types.
